metadata.name: ZOOM (4hwf) #70
metadata.description:
"Das System lässt keine Überraschung zu. Überraschung ist Ausdruck partieller Unkenntnis. Überraschung weist auf Mangel von Wissen hin, deutet Leerstellen an. Bisher hatten sie keine Leerstellen festgestellt." — Herbert W. Franke

ZOOM explores linear scaling of pixels by zooming into a noisy framebuffer. Various zoom levels are composed one below the other, becoming a cinematic animation through iteration of the hue values of the pixels in the framebuffer.

In Gedenken an Herbert W. Franke
Kim Asendorf, 2022

objkt.name: Hexagones - Art for Bots for Humans #57

metadata.name: Hexagones - Art for Bots for Humans #57
metadata.description:
For (some) Humans.

"Hexagones - Art for Bots for Humans", also known as "HAFBFH", "HA4B4H", and "HA4B4HY2KLIGCVEd" (Hexagones - Art For Bots For Humans Y2K Light Industries Genesis Contract Variable Edition), is an airdrop project for owners (who I assume are all bots) of the original "Hexagones - Art for Bots" project, a snapshot of which was taken at 4:30 pm on a sunny Monday afternoon, 14th March 2022 in the UK.

This is a prequel to "Hexagones - Art for Bots for Humans for Print" project, which will appear on the new contract, following a similar but not identical design.

Instructions ::

1-0, '-' & '=' - Toggle various layers
m - Toggle the main hexagon
f - 30 second Flickr mode
h - Toggle high-res
s - Download and save image

issuer.name: Hexagones - Art for Bots for Humans

metadata.name: Mercalli #66
metadata.description:
"Earthquakes don't kill people, buildings do." – Unknown

Earthquakes are not just natural disasters, but also human tragedies. They can strike suddenly and violently, leaving behind a trail of destruction and trauma that can last a lifetime. For survivors, the experience of an earthquake can be life changing.

Homes and neighborhoods are reduced to rubble, and the familiar landmarks and memories of a lifetime are wiped away in an instant. In the aftermath of an earthquake, survivors must grapple with the emotional toll of their loss, the uncertainty of the future, and the challenge of rebuilding their lives.

The first step after a disaster is to provide emergency medical care, shelter, and basic necessities like food and water. Once these immediate needs are met, attention can turn to assessing the damage and developing a plan for reconstruction.

The rebuilding process can be a long and challenging journey, as it involves not only repairing physical damage but also restoring the social fabric of affected communities, addressing issues like housing, infrastructure, and economic recovery, as well as addressing the psychological and emotional impact of the disaster on individuals and communities.

It is crucial to rebuild using proper techniques and investing in the right resources to prevent similar disasters from happening in the future. Reconstruction must be done with a focus on resilience, sustainability, and safety. This means taking into consideration the local conditions, the latest research on earthquake-resistant materials and designs, and the needs of the affected communities.

The cost of not investing in proper reconstruction after an earthquake can be immense. Poorly constructed buildings and infrastructure can increase the risk of further damage in the event of another earthquake, leading to more loss of life and property. On the other hand, investing in proper reconstruction can help create a safer and more resilient environment, reducing the impact of future earthquakes and protecting the lives and livelihoods of those who call the affected region home.

For this reason, it is crucial that regions impacted by earthquakes receive immediate and adequate economic and technical support, and that available resources are managed with proper planning and great responsibility.

This generative art project explores the destructive power of earthquakes and seeks to capture the raw emotions of those who have experienced earthquakes and the transformation of their world into a memory.

100% of the primary split is going to @tezquakeaid, Tezos community initiative providing aid to the victims of the catastrophic earthquake that struck Turkey and Syria on February 6, 2023. 
Once again the Tezos community has proven to be special. 💚

Hand coded after midnight.
Powered by p5, matter & chroma.

======> COMMANDS (In Live Mode) <======

Press ‘s’ to save the current output.
The generation takes some time but you can see the simulation and drawing process happening in real-time.

Settings are passed as URL parameters and pressing the following keys, the artwork will regenerate at a different scale:

- 1 -> 1.920x1.080
- 2 -> 4.000x2.252
- 3 -> 6.000x3.378
- 4 -> 8.000x4.504

=============> MAKING OF <=============

https://www.fxhash.xyz/article/the-making-of-mercalli

metadata.name: Symmetry and Intricacy #83
metadata.description:
Symmetry and intricacy.
Structure shaping light.
Beauty in a stark winter.

Symmetry and Intricacy grows unique snowflakes and illuminates them in varying colours. The shape of each snowflake is a reflection of the environment it grew in; the temperature, humidity and pressure all impacts its growth as it is falling through the atmosphere.

metadata.name: Kinetic Bug #225
metadata.description:
Best viewed on a decent GPU,  because physics simulations are performance intensive. Otherwise you might encounter a bug...

In a world of digital data, a new life is born. We are not alone...
Explore how kinetic bugs make their way through the world. What will their destiny be?
It's curious that we can sympathize to a predefined creature living it's predestined life in the digital world. Coding in the computer abyss reveals interesting worlds.

Keyboard
[ space ] - jump
[ click ] - increase velocity briefly
[ r ] - restart simulation
[ s ] - save image
[ f ] - full screen

Mobile
[ two finger tap ] - jump
[ tap ] - increase velocity briefly

Created with Three.js + rapier.js
Copyright (c) July 2022 Shaderism

metadata.name: On the sea, I ... #112
metadata.description:
Generative painting based on churning waves with floating buoys. Represented in three different media.

Features:
- Three drawing styles: "watercolor" 70%, "brush" 30%, or "block" 70%
- Warm white or blue waves
- Variable border and column sizes
- Number of columns
- Calm waves at the edges or center
- Color 80% or red 20% buoys
- Buoy drawing style, "watercolor" or "brush"

Viewing in desktop Chrome is recommended for the best results.
Press ’s’ to save a screenshot. Image size is 928x1200px
Press ’p’ to pause/resume.

Add '&size=#' to the end of the live view URL to render a variation of your edition at a resolution up to 3,000 px. 

Copyright (c) 2022 Matt Perkins, hello@mattperkins.me, @nudoru on Twitter. Licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, see LICENSE.txt for more information.

metadata.name: Breeding Grounds #240
metadata.description:
Social Networks.
Molecular Clouds.
Neuronal Circuitries.
Quantum Mechanical Structures.

Blurring Boundaries.
Infinite Self-Similarities.

Total.
Mess.

Perfect.
Order.

/////
Each element (besides the painting ground textures) is based on the underlying subdivided grid which is sometimes exposed like some kind of preliminary drawing.

Note on the split:
After seeing some of the outputs, Sermin had a dream about “Breeding Grounds” and I decided to incorporate events and feelings from her dream into the project. That's how the names of the color palettes came about. Thank you for naming them, Sermin!

Press 's' to save a .png with the current resolution (2000x3000).
If you want a file with higher resolution and/or without the “Painting Ground Textures” for printing please contact me via Twitter or Discord. 

/////
Created with p5.js

Twitter: @serminmin / @pxlshrd

metadata.name: Orchard #352
metadata.description:
A natural media rendering of a fruit tree during one of the four seasons. Sometimes, you'll catch it at night - with lights strung on the tree!

The amount of color in spring and fall, snow in winter, fruit on and off the tree will all vary. There are four spring bloom palettes, four trunk colors, and seven types of fruit. 

Press ’s’ to save a screenshot. Image size is 1000x1000px
Press ’p’ to pause/resume.

To render a variation at a new size, open live view and append '?size=#' to the end of the URL. The new size may be from 100 to 3,000. This version will not be a 1:1 match to your edition but will have all of the same features.

Copyright (c) 2022 Matt Perkins, hello@mattperkins.me, @nudoru on Twitter. Licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, see LICENSE.txt for more information.

metadata.name: Aeoflu #253
metadata.description:
This broadcast is transmitted to the brains of the humanoid race from the planet Xiurq during hyperspace travel. The algorithm is specifically designed to preserve the cognitive functions of the species during "slow time" and overload. The use of the algorithm by other races has not been studied. Prohibited for use outside specially certified laboratories by intergalactic convention 30292-HD-92 

The idea of this project is infinite motion, but it can be stopped (“space”) and save static image ("s" button)

There are three modes. They are switched by click. Each switch changes the input parameters, so there are quite a few options

!!!Flashing lights seizure warning!!!
